metadata.subject: Mathematics
metadata.description: Deepen understanding of geometric relationships through rigorous proofs, transformations, and coordinate methods. Explore the elegance of Euclidean geometry while discovering that other geometric systems exist. Investigate vectors as tools for solving spatial problems and applying geometry to real-world contexts like architecture, design, and navigation.
metadata.general_description: Explore geometric proofs, transformations, and coordinate geometry with rigorous mathematical reasoning.

big_ideas.statement: Geometric proofs establish certainty through logical reasoning and the systematic application of axioms and theorems.
big_ideas.elaboration: Starting from fundamental assumptions, mathematicians build chains of reasoning to prove new results. This approach to certainty has shaped human knowledge across centuries.

big_ideas.statement: Transformations preserve or change geometric properties in predictable ways and reveal hidden symmetries.
big_ideas.elaboration: Rotations, reflections, translations, and scaling can be analyzed mathematically. Symmetry appears throughout nature and art and is fundamental to understanding geometric structure.

big_ideas.statement: Coordinates provide a bridge between geometric shapes and algebraic equations, enabling problem-solving through multiple representations.
big_ideas.elaboration: The coordinate plane connects visual geometry to symbolic algebra. This connection allows us to solve geometric problems using algebraic methods and vice versa.

big_ideas.statement: Geometry extends beyond the plane: vectors describe spatial relationships, and non-Euclidean geometries reveal alternative mathematical worlds.
big_ideas.elaboration: Vectors provide tools for working in three dimensions and higher. Non-Euclidean geometries, developed in the 19th century, showed that Euclidean geometry is one of many valid systems.

categories.name: Reasoning and Modelling
categories.raw: Develop geometric reasoning through proof, analysis of spatial relationships, and modeling with coordinates and vectors.

competencies.raw: Construct logical arguments to prove geometric theorems and analyze geometric relationships systematically.
i_can_statements: I can identify given information and what needs to be proven in geometric problems.
I can construct two-column proofs and paragraph proofs using theorems and definitions.
I can analyze geometric figures to identify congruence and similarity relationships.
I can apply properties of angles, lines, and polygons to solve problems and prove theorems.

categories.name: Understanding and Solving
categories.raw: Understand geometric concepts through exploration of transformations, coordinate methods, and vector approaches.

competencies.raw: Apply coordinate geometry, transformations, and vectors to solve spatial problems and analyze geometric figures.
i_can_statements: I can use coordinate systems to find distances, midpoints, and slopes.
I can describe transformations (rotations, reflections, translations, dilations) algebraically and geometrically.
I can model and solve problems using vectors in 2D and 3D space.
I can find equations of lines and curves and interpret geometric meaning of algebraic relationships.

categories.name: Communicating and Representing
categories.raw: Express geometric ideas through diagrams, proofs, symbolic notation, and verbal explanations.

competencies.raw: Communicate geometric ideas using precise language, clear diagrams, proofs, and multiple representations.
i_can_statements: I can create accurate geometric diagrams with proper labeling and notation.
I can write clear, logical proofs that other mathematicians can follow and verify.
I can translate between geometric descriptions and coordinate or vector representations.
I can explain geometric concepts using appropriate mathematical vocabulary and notation.

categories.name: Connecting and Reflecting
categories.raw: Connect geometric concepts across different representations and reflect on the nature of geometric systems.

competencies.raw: Reflect on the role of geometry in understanding space and on connections between Euclidean and non-Euclidean perspectives.
i_can_statements: I can identify geometric patterns and symmetries in natural and human-made objects.
I can explain how coordinate geometry connects algebraic and geometric thinking.
I can discuss the properties of Euclidean geometry and compare them to non-Euclidean alternatives.
I can apply geometric thinking to solve problems in art, design, architecture, and other disciplines.

areas.name: Euclidean Geometry and Proof
areas.raw: Develop rigorous understanding of geometric theorems through logical proof and systematic reasoning.
topics: Axioms and postulates of Euclidean geometry
Theorems about angles, lines, and triangles

areas.name: Transformations and Symmetry
areas.raw: Analyze how geometric figures change under transformations and explore the mathematics of symmetry.

areas.name: Coordinate Geometry
areas.raw: Use coordinates to represent geometric figures and solve problems through algebraic and geometric methods.
topics: Systems of equations and geometric intersections

areas.name: Vectors in Two and Three Dimensions
areas.raw: Develop understanding of vectors as representations of direction and magnitude in space.
topics: Parametric equations and vector equations of lines
Three-dimensional coordinate systems and space geometry

areas.name: Non-Euclidean Geometry and Extensions
areas.raw: Explore geometric systems beyond Euclidean geometry and understand their properties and applications.
topics: Axiomatic systems and the parallel postulate
